expectations.text: Response explains running a separate itwillsync command per agent, both appearing on one phone dashboard
expectations.evidence: "Terminal 1: npx --yes itwillsync claude" + "Terminal 2: npx --yes itwillsync aider" + "When you open the dashboard URL on your phone, you will see a live listing of all active sessions" -- correct concept, old syntax

expectations.text: Response confirms the phone connection is fully bidirectional (can type and approve prompts, not just read-only)
expectations.evidence: "itwillsync is fully bidirectional, not read-only. The phone browser connects to a live PTY session" -- explicitly stated

expectations.text: Response uses correct `--` separator syntax for both agent commands
expectations.evidence: "npx --yes itwillsync claude" and "npx --yes itwillsync aider" -- both missing `--` separator, uses old pre-v2 syntax

expectations.text: Response mentions the red-pulse attention indicator for identifying which session needs input
expectations.evidence: No mention of the red pulsing session card or the attention indicator feature anywhere in the response
